REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC, DOMUS MANAGEMENT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO VIOLATION OF CC 1942.5 AND FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT NOTES THAT NO CASES WITH SIMILAR FACTS WERE CITED IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER. THE ONLY CITED CASE (MAHAN V. CHARLES W. CHAN INS. AGENCY, INC., 14 CAL.APP.5TH 841) IS FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE AND PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT DOES NOT CONTAIN FACTS THAT WOULD BRING THEIR CLAIMS WITHIN THE REASONING OF MAHAN USED TO FIND APPLICABILITY OF THE ELDER ABUSE STATUTE TO THE PROPERTY RIGHTS OF THE AGGRIEVED PARTY. FURTHERMORE, IF PLAINTIFF'S ELDER ABUSE CLAIM IS BASED IN FRAUD, ALL FACTS MUST BE STATED WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC, DOMUS MANAGEMENT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts giving rise to violation of CC 1942.5 and Financial Elder Abuse cause of action. The Court notes that no cases with similar facts were cited in opposition to the demurrer. The only cited case (Mahan v. Charles W. Chan Ins. Agency, Inc., 14 Cal.App.5th 841) is factually distinguishable and Plaintiff's complaint does not contain facts that would bring their claims within the reasoning of Mahan used to find applicability of the Elder Abuse statute to the property rights of the aggrieved party. Furthermore, if Plaintiff's Elder Abuse claim is based in fraud, all facts must be stated with requisite specificity. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Shelby A. Kennick
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC, DOMUS MANAGEMENT Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint
is moot in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is moot regarding the request for attorney's fees in light of ruling on the demurrer. Motion is granted as to the request for treble damages, for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each element of CC 3345.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
